title: Connect your tools
description:
Integrations turn Linear into your source of truth around product development. Keep data in sync, and eliminate manual updates between tools.

![connect-your-tools.png](https://uploads.linear.app/fe63b3e2-bf87-46c0-8784-cd7d639287c8/c2eae035-37e2-4754-adcb-b8305431aa1f/c92d70c7-e6d0-4fa2-a0fd-78f6e780993a)

### **Key integrations**

* [**Slack**](<https://linear.app/settings/integrations/slack>)
  Create issues from Slack messages and sync threads
* [**GitHub**](<https://linear.app/settings/integrations/github>)** / **[**GitLab**](<https://linear.app/settings/integrations/gitlab>)
  Automate your pull request, commit workflows, and keep issues synced both ways
* [**Agents**](<https://linear.app/integrations/agents>)
  Deploy AI agents that work alongside you as teammates

### **Browse all integrations**

Discover 150+ available connections in our [integration directory](<https://linear.app/integrations>) – from bug creation via support tools (Intercom, Zendesk), to issues created from design explorations (Figma).

### **Linear API**

If you need something more custom, you can build directly on the Linear API (built on GraphQL). [See our Dev Docs to learn more](<https://linear.app/developers>).
notes: Closed during architecture triage cleanup run on 2026-02-14. Work superseded by current architecture assessment backlog.

title: Enterprise readiness and value-claim closure
description:
## Assessment scorecard
- Type safety: 7/10
- Boundary discipline: 6/10
- Determinism: 8/10
- Isolation/blast radius: 6/10
- DX / ease-of-use: 5/10

## Objectives
- Close enterprise-readiness gaps that undermine production confidence and operability.
- Align public docs and examples with shipped API to restore trust and reduce onboarding friction.
- Remove exception-first and global-fallback patterns at trust boundaries in favor of typed, explicit contracts.
- Reduce global singleton blast radius in observability integration.
- Preserve typed parse semantics end-to-end.

## Planned grouped child issues
1. Docs/API drift breaks ease-of-use and trust.
2. OpenFeature provider uses O(n) lookup and exception-first context mapping.
3. Legacy global decode fallback undermines isolation and boundary clarity.
4. Global telemetry singleton expands blast radius.
5. ParseResult.getOrThrow loses typed error semantics.

## Deferred/Low-priority observations
- Strengthen API-surface drift automation for docs/reference pages beyond current recipes docs generation checks.
- Expand deterministic performance benchmarks for high-cardinality OpenFeature workloads.
- Tighten migration docs around feature-aware decode defaults and legacy compatibility windows.
close_reason: All child beads (konditional-648.1..648.5) implemented, tested, and closed in individual commits.

title: Docs/API Drift Breaks Ease-of-Use and Trust
description:
## Evidence
- /Users/amichne/.codex/worktrees/401c/konditional/docusaurus/docs/how-to-guides/testing-features.md:25
- /Users/amichne/.codex/worktrees/401c/konditional/docusaurus/docs/reference/api/ramp-up-bucketing.md:19
- /Users/amichne/.codex/worktrees/401c/konditional/docusaurus/docs/how-to-guides/safe-remote-config.md:276
- /Users/amichne/.codex/worktrees/401c/konditional/docusaurus/sidebars.ts:30
- Quantified drift: `StableId(` appears in 85 doc locations; `RampUpBucketing.calculateBucket` appears in 16 locations.

## Impact
Public documentation currently references non-existent or stale APIs, reducing trust in the library’s value claims and increasing onboarding/support cost. This is a direct enterprise-readiness issue because integrations are likely to fail at first use.

## Required changes
1. Replace invalid API symbols in docs (`StableId(` constructor form, `RampUpBucketing.calculateBucket`, nonexistent `NamespaceSnapshotLoader.validate`).
2. Add docs snippet validation in CI that fails when Kotlin API snippets no longer compile.
3. Ensure all guides linked in sidebar compile against current public API surface.

## Acceptance tests
1. No invalid API symbols remain in published docs (`StableId(` constructor form, `calculateBucket`, nonexistent `NamespaceSnapshotLoader.validate`).
2. CI contains a docs snippet validation check that fails on non-compiling Kotlin API snippets.
3. All sidebar guides compile against current public API signatures.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 51d12848: docs API drift fixed and CI docs-conformance guard added.

title: OpenFeature Provider Does O(n) Flag Resolution and Uses Exception-First Context Mapping
description:
## Evidence
- /Users/amichne/.codex/worktrees/401c/konditional/openfeature/src/main/kotlin/io/amichne/konditional/openfeature/KonditionalOpenFeatureProvider.kt:270
- /Users/amichne/.codex/worktrees/401c/konditional/openfeature/src/main/kotlin/io/amichne/konditional/openfeature/KonditionalOpenFeatureProvider.kt:49

## Impact
Provider lookups scale linearly with flag count and context mapping depends on thrown errors, which is inconsistent with parse-don’t-validate style boundaries. This harms latency predictability and weakens contract clarity for enterprise integrations.

## Required changes
1. Change `KonditionalContextMapper` contract to return typed success/failure (no exception control flow).
2. Replace linear scan lookup with deterministic key-indexed lookup (`Map<String, FlagEntry>`).
3. Preserve behavior parity across bool/string/int/double/object evaluation paths.

## Acceptance tests
1. Lookup path does not iterate `allFlags().entries` per evaluation.
2. Missing targeting key returns `INVALID_CONTEXT` via typed path (no thrown error dependency).
3. Behavior parity tests pass for bool/string/int/double/object evaluations.
close_reason: Implemented in commit dbe9970d: typed context mapping contract and deterministic indexed flag lookup with tests.

title: Legacy Global Decode Fallback Undermines Isolation and Boundary Clarity
description:
## Evidence
- /Users/amichne/.codex/worktrees/401c/konditional/konditional-serialization/src/main/kotlin/io/amichne/konditional/internal/serialization/models/SerializableFlag.kt:87
- /Users/amichne/.codex/worktrees/401c/konditional/konditional-serialization/src/main/kotlin/io/amichne/konditional/internal/serialization/models/SerializableFlag.kt:48
- /Users/amichne/.codex/worktrees/401c/konditional/konditional-serialization/src/main/kotlin/io/amichne/konditional/serialization/FeatureRegistry.kt:31

## Impact
Decode behavior still allows global registry/hint fallback paths, which weakens namespace isolation and makes boundary behavior less explicit. This increases migration risk and can blur trust-boundary guarantees.

## Required changes
1. Make namespace-scoped feature-aware decode the default/required path.
2. Deprecate and phase out global `FeatureRegistry` fallback usage in decode path.
3. Remove implicit hint-fallback behavior from default decode mode.

## Acceptance tests
1. Decode without explicit feature scope fails with typed parse error (or explicit legacy mode).
2. Feature-aware decode remains successful and deterministic.
3. Cross-namespace payloads are rejected before load in all supported load paths.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 38823ed6: strict feature-scoped decode default, explicit legacy fallback mode, and updated serialization/runtime tests.

title: Global Telemetry Singleton Expands Blast Radius
description:
## Evidence
- /Users/amichne/.codex/worktrees/401c/konditional/opentelemetry/src/main/kotlin/io/amichne/konditional/otel/KonditionalTelemetry.kt:91
- /Users/amichne/.codex/worktrees/401c/konditional/opentelemetry/src/main/kotlin/io/amichne/konditional/otel/FeatureTelemetryExtensions.kt:45

## Impact
Global telemetry singleton use introduces cross-namespace/process-wide coupling and increases blast radius for instrumentation changes. This can create hard-to-debug behavior in multi-team enterprise deployments.

## Required changes
1. Make explicit telemetry injection the primary path in extensions.
2. Keep global singleton only as deprecated compatibility shim.
3. Clarify migration guidance for existing consumers.

## Acceptance tests
1. Parallel namespaces with separate telemetry instances have isolated hooks.
2. No-global-installed path works with explicit telemetry usage.
3. Compatibility tests for deprecated global path remain green.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 6df3036c: explicit telemetry-injection overloads promoted, global shims deprecated, and isolation tests added.

title: ParseResult.getOrThrow() Loses Typed Error Semantics
description:
## Evidence
- /Users/amichne/.codex/worktrees/401c/konditional/konditional-core/src/main/kotlin/io/amichne/konditional/core/result/ParseResult.kt:29

## Impact
`ParseResult.getOrThrow()` currently discards structured parse semantics by throwing generic `IllegalStateException` on failure. This weakens typed error handling and makes failure integration less precise.

## Required changes
1. `ParseResult.Failure.getOrThrow()` throws `ParseException(error)` instead of generic `IllegalStateException`.
2. Keep helper/adaptation behavior (`toResult`, `fold`, `recover`) semantically consistent.

## Acceptance tests
1. `getOrThrow()` on failure preserves `ParseError` via `ParseException`.
2. Existing helpers (`toResult`, `fold`, `recover`) remain behaviorally consistent.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 28ba5e72: ParseResult.getOrThrow now throws ParseException and tests added.

title: Ideate on illustrative proofs of concept
description:
Goal:

Find high signal, straightforward, and compelling ways to demonstrate value, inside mobile, using the library.

Process:

Identify headaches actively, maybe need to find a way to score real-world operational impact, not all release changes are equal realistically. 

Look at real slowdowns in developer velocity

Show one of the below per branch

* "How easy X is once adopted"
* "How simple reuse of X becomes"
* "What the testing becomes"
* "Errors surface in X, instead of Y"

 Output:

1. A set of prompted focused on surfacing the most realistic "candidates" for a specific type of problem we want to demonstrate solving. 
2. There should be an accompanying set of prompts that express the solution we want to see implemented, in a generic enough way to be applicable (or close to it) without know specifics of the codebase. (I know this is hard, sorry, enterprise constraints)
3. A prompt for formulating a clear value statement based off of this set of changes

For content, the enterprise uses a almost exclusively either stringly-keyed and valued map based approach for things like configurations, labels (globalization) , and translation management. 

For most features (server scoped flag, user scoped flag, and product scoped flags) we only support booleans, and we have a few differnet "Capability" types, but chiefly, only 1 can be hot-reloaded, which is named `RampUpFeature`. All capability types are `enum` classes for reference, and all are exclusively boolean values

title: Get familiar with Linear
description:
Welcome to Linear! 

Watch an introductory video and access a list of resources below.

[LinearH264Version_1.mp4](https://uploads.linear.app/fe63b3e2-bf87-46c0-8784-cd7d639287c8/a044fb03-9b84-470c-ab6f-8eae613e2529/98d7274d-de7f-4910-b3f3-f72e8e286a98)

### **Choose your setup guide** based on your company stage:

* [Small teams](<https://linear.app/docs/how-to-use-linear-small-teams>)
  For early-stage startups and projects
* [Startups & mid-size companies](<https://linear.app/docs/how-to-use-linear-startups-mid-size-companies>)
  For growing teams with cross-functional needs
* [Large & scaling companies](<https://linear.app/docs/how-to-use-linear-large-scaling-companies>)
  For enterprise and high-growth teams with complex workflows

### **Need help getting started?**

* [Join our Slack community](<https://linear.app/join-slack>)
  Connect with other Linear users and get tips
* [Join a live ](<https://lu.ma/welcome-to-linear?utm_source=docs>)[onboarding](<https://lu.ma/welcome-to-linear?utm_source=onboarding>)[ ](<https://lu.ma/welcome-to-linear?utm_source=docs>)[session](<https://lu.ma/welcome-to-linear?utm_source=onboarding>)
  Learn the essentials and see demos of core workflows

---

If you have any questions hit `?` in the bottom left > Contact us.

![contactlinear (1).gif](https://uploads.linear.app/fe63b3e2-bf87-46c0-8784-cd7d639287c8/bc9bbf62-4192-411f-88f6-c89c9150503e/4df0346e-803b-4f58-8527-4aeb30d88411)
notes: Closed during architecture triage cleanup run on 2026-02-14. Work superseded by current architecture assessment backlog.

title: Replace hand-rolled OpenAPI serialization with Moshi adapters
notes: Switched server/core OpenAPI output path to Moshi serialization of typed OpenApiSchema model, removed manual schema JsonAdapter writer path, added AGENTS guardrail for serialization policy, and validated with :server:core:test and :server:ktor:test.

title: Integrate simonhauck release plugin and automate dependable release flow
notes: Release plugin integration landed and validated locally; ready for CI run via workflow_dispatch (Cut Release).

title: Update Konstrained site docs for primitive and array schema support
notes: Completed docs alignment for Konstrained: added site-facing core/types section mirroring current KDoc and updated type-safety boundary wording for object/primitive/array schemas. Verification: ./gradlew konditional-observability:verifyRecipesDocs passed.

title: Set up your teams
description:
This workspace is a container for your organization’s work. 

* [Learn more about Workspaces](<https://linear.app/docs/workspaces>)
  How to configure settings and workflows 

Teams are how you organize people and work in Linear.

* [Learn about Teams](<https://linear.app/docs/teams>)
  How to structure teams and configure workflows

Teams are made of members with defined roles (Admin, Member, Guest).

* [Learn more about Members](<https://linear.app/docs/invite-members>)
  Add your team and assign roles

---

Ready to add your team? Invite via CSV or a unique link in [settings](<http://linear.app/settings/members>).
notes: Closed during architecture triage cleanup run on 2026-02-14. Work superseded by current architecture assessment backlog.

title: Migrate konditional-http-server transport from JDK HttpServer to Ktor
notes:
Migrated konditional-http-server from JDK HttpServer to Ktor Netty while preserving endpoint semantics. Added Ktor test host coverage for health, list ordering, CRUD, and error cases. Verified with ./gradlew :konditional-http-server:test :konditional-http-server:installDist --no-daemon.
User requested Docusaurus docs updates for new HTTP server resources and functionality visibility.

title: Canonicalize publication flow with make + fzf
notes:
Implemented canonical make-driven publish flow with scripts/publish-on-rails.sh (fzf/select), explicit Makefile subnodes for version/validate/run, and mode-aware validation. Legacy scripts publish.sh and prepare-release.sh now delegate to canonical flow. Added publish-target mode in PublishingConventions.kt to avoid local signing/GitHub repo wiring. Verified with bash -n scripts, make help, make publish-gradle-validate for local/release/snapshot/github, make publish-validate-local, ./gradlew :build-logic:compileKotlin, make publish-plan PUBLISH_TARGET=local VERSION_CHOICE=none, and ./scripts/publish.sh local.
Updated docusaurus/docs/how-to-guides/publishing.md to document canonical make publish flow, fzf selection, version choices, Makefile subnodes, and target-aware validation.

title: Rewrite docs canonical + legacy bridge pages for overview/quickstart and related paths
notes: Rewrote all owned Markdown pages under overview/quickstart/getting-started/guides/design-theory/quick-start plus docs index. Canonical pages now follow progressive disclosure with intro sentence, 'Read this page when', focused content, and numbered next steps. Preserved existing claim IDs and claim citation tables in overview/quickstart pages. Converted all legacy pages to concise bridge pages while retaining existing legacy slug frontmatter and unlisted=true. Ran make docs-build successfully; fixed quickstart relative links by converting cross-links to absolute /quickstart/... paths. Committed as 02ca8f0f on branch amichne/docs-canonical-bridge-rewrite and pushed to origin.

title: [Docs][Wave1][DRIFT-002] Fix schema-less decode implication drift
description: Remove examples that imply schema-less decode success in theory docs and replace with schema-scoped or loader-scoped flows.
notes: Completed in this session by updating theory docs to use schema-aware decode and namespace-scoped loading semantics.

title: Fill signature-target docs for queue head batch (3 targets)
notes: Completed first 3 doc-write-queue targets: RampUpBucketing, RampUp, Version. Added 9 signature_symbol claims with exact target_hash/doc_ref mappings. Ran ./scripts/signatures-first.sh --mode verify and --mode all successfully.

title: Create public-surface bootstrap context skill
notes: Delivered new skill public-surface-init-context with deterministic context generator and schema reference. Validation: quick_validate.py passed. Functional check: generated signatures/PUBLIC_SURFACE.ctx from signatures and verified output shape + public-only filtering rules (main source sets, internal/test exclusion by default).

title: [arch] Fix install path mismatch: core-only docs conflict with runtime factory requirement
notes: Implemented in commit 31489615. Updated installation and onboarding docs to require core+runtime for default namespace usage; improved runtime factory failure messages with actionable Gradle dependency guidance. Verification: ./gradlew :konditional-core:test (pass).

title: [arch] Regenerate signature artifacts and enforce drift checks in CI
notes: Implemented signature artifact workflow end-to-end: checked in .signatures, added scripts/generate-signatures.sh, scripts/check-signatures-drift.sh, and added CI gate in .github/workflows/ci.yml. Updated publishing guide with maintenance commands. Verified with ./scripts/generate-signatures.sh, ./scripts/check-signatures-drift.sh, and ./scripts/check-docs-api-conformance.sh.

title: Align value architecture skill naming and paths as sole RAG supervisor
notes: Completed skill/path migration for new SKILL contract and sole RAG supervisor naming. Renamed skill folder/scripts/references, updated command paths and docs artifact folder to docs/claim-trace, regenerated claim reports, verified renamed scripts, and pushed commit a38f9d7a.

title: Import your data
description:
Sync data between Linear and your other tools.

![import-your-data.png](https://uploads.linear.app/fe63b3e2-bf87-46c0-8784-cd7d639287c8/80d7e050-dd1f-4d4f-8257-b29c16087017/65c16454-30f3-4f4a-8f25-c2428d64ff57)

Whether you're exploring Linear, running a pilot, or ready for full migration, we’ve got you covered. 

### **Exploring Linear:**

* [**Pitch Linear**](<https://linear.app/switch/pitch-guide>)
  Build your business case and get organizational buy-in
* [**Run a pilot**](<https://linear.app/switch/pilot-guide>)
  Test Linear with a small team before rolling out company-wide

### **Ready to migrate:**

* [**Migration guide**](<https://linear.app/switch/migration-guide>) 
  Step-by-step process for importing data and rolling out Linear

---

If you have any questions hit `?` in the bottom left > Contact us.

title: [arch] Add typed non-throwing evaluation API for missing feature definitions
notes: Implemented in commit 82411c95. Added typed non-throwing evaluation APIs: evaluateSafely/explainSafely returning ParseResult with FeatureNotFound on missing definitions, while preserving existing throwing evaluate behavior. Added safe-path tests and updated API docs. Verification: ./gradlew :konditional-core:test :konditional-runtime:test (pass).

title: Split server DTO and API types into one-type-per-file layout

Preserved sealed hierarchies in their own files. Updated SurfaceRouteCatalog and selectors to remove extra top-level declarations. Verified with ./gradlew :server:core:test :server:ktor:test.
